name: Coombe Bissett
overview: Coombe Bissett is a village in the River Ebble valley about 3-4 miles south-west of Salisbury, on the A354 towards Blandford Forum, with a population of around 675 (2011 census). It lies within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape and has a parish church, community-run shop with Post Office, primary school and the Fox and Goose pub. The River Ebble runs through the village, and many period homes (several listed) sit near the water.
character: A picturesque, strongly community-minded chalk-valley village with listed period properties around a village green, surrounded by downland and footpaths.
amenities: Community-run village shop with Post Office
Salisbury and South Wiltshire Golf Course (north of the village)
Coombe Bissett Down Nature Reserve (Wiltshire Wildlife Trust)

schools.name: Secondary/grammar schools in Salisbury (Bishop Wordsworth's, South Wilts Grammar)

prices.summary: Sought-after Ebble valley village with a mix of listed period cottages and family homes; proximity to Salisbury and Cranborne Chase supports values. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Coombe Bissett Down Nature Reserve (chalk downland, rare butterflies)
River Ebble valley walks and footpaths
Golf at Salisbury and South Wiltshire Golf Course
pros: Strong community with shop, pub, school and clubs
Close to Salisbury (~10 min) and the hospital
Good walking and cycling on the doorstep
cons: No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Sits on the A354 with through traffic
Limited services compared with larger villages
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quintessential chalk-valley village with strong community and quick Salisbury access.
primarySupermarket: Community village shop; larger supermarkets in Salisbury

sources.label: Coombe Bissett CofE Primary School - GOV.UK
